name: MV-SAM3D
readme_summary: MV-SAM3D is a multi-view 3D reconstruction framework that enhances the SAM 3D Objects model by utilizing multiple viewpoints. It aims to produce more stable and consistent 3D geometry, textures, and scene compositions for both single and multiple objects.
key_features: Aims for stable geometry and texture
primary_use_case: Generating more robust and consistent 3D object reconstructions from multiple input images.
cover_image_prompt: A sculptor carefully chiseling a 3D statue from a block of marble. Instead of traditional tools, the sculptor uses multiple beams of light, each originating from a different angle (representing multi-view images), converging on the marble to precisely shape it. The marble block glows with intricate details as it forms. In the background, a holographic display shows the different camera angles feeding into the process, and the repository name 'MV-SAM3D' is subtly etched onto the sculptor's workbench. The scene is set in a clean, modern workshop with soft, focused lighting, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clarity and detail.

name: graphrag-workbench
description: Interactive 3D visualization of knowledge graphs generated by Microsoft GraphRAG. Explore entities, relationships, and communities with immersive visual analytics.
readme_summary: GraphRAG Workbench is an interactive web application for building and visualizing knowledge graphs from documents using Microsoft's GraphRAG framework. It offers advanced AI-powered analysis, natural language querying, and immersive 3D exploration of extracted entities and relationships.
primary_use_case: To provide an interactive and visually intuitive platform for users to build, explore, and query knowledge graphs derived from their documents using AI.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a vast, ancient library where the bookshelves are made of glowing data streams and the books are translucent cubes containing interconnected nodes. The librarian uses a magical magnifying glass to reveal hidden relationships and communities within the data, which are visualized as shimmering constellations. Subtle UI elements resembling a modern dashboard are integrated into the library's architecture, showing document uploads and AI analysis progress. The overall mood is one of discovery and intellectual exploration, rendered in a detailed, ethereal digital painting style with a focus on luminous blues and golds.

name: Stable-Video-Infinity
description: [ICLR 26 Oral] Stable Video Infinity: Infinite-Length Video Generation with Error Recycling
readme_summary: Stable Video Infinity (SVI) is a Python-based framework for generating infinitely long videos by recycling errors. It builds upon diffusion transformer models to achieve this, offering advanced capabilities for various video generation tasks.
key_features: Supports various applications like end-to-end filming, audio-driven talking faces, and dance generation
Available in different versions (SVI 1.0, SVI 2.0, SVI 2.0 Pro) and integrated with ComfyUI workflows
primary_use_case: Generating exceptionally long and coherent videos by iteratively refining frames and recycling previous errors, enabling applications like continuous storytelling, realistic character animation, and extended visual sequences.
cover_image_prompt: A majestic, ancient loom weaving an endless tapestry of moving images. The loom's threads are streams of digital data, flowing from a source of raw visual information. As the tapestry extends infinitely into the distance, subtle glitches or 'errors' are visibly recycled back into the loom's mechanism, transforming into new, coherent visual elements. Small, glowing UI elements representing 'error recycling' and 'infinite generation' are subtly integrated into the loom's design. The scene is set in a bright, ethereal workshop with a soft, digital glow. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a vibrant, yet harmonious color palette, emphasizing the seamless continuation of the video.

name: prek
description: ⚡ A fast Git hook manager written in Rust, designed as a drop-in alternative to pre-commit, reimagined.
readme_summary: Prek is a fast, dependency-free Git hook manager written in Rust, serving as a drop-in alternative to pre-commit. It offers improved performance, reduced disk space usage, and enhanced features like monorepo support and integrated Python environment management.
key_features: Faster and more efficient than pre-commit
Integration with uv for Python environment management
Improved toolchain installations for multiple languages
primary_use_case: Managing and running Git hooks efficiently in software development projects, providing a faster and more streamlined alternative to pre-commit.
cover_image_prompt: A highly efficient, futuristic robot arm meticulously organizing and checking small, standardized packages (representing Git commits) on a conveyor belt. Each package passes through a glowing scanner that instantly verifies its contents, with successful packages receiving a green checkmark and faulty ones a red 'X'. The robot arm is labeled 'prek' and subtle lines of code or data streams flow around the scene. The background is a clean, modern workshop with a focus on speed and precision. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, clean color palette, emphasizing efficiency and automation.

name: flowsint
description: A modern platform for visual, flexible, and extensible graph-based investigations. For cybersecurity analysts and investigators.
readme_summary: Flowsint is an open-source OSINT graph exploration tool designed for ethical cybersecurity investigations. It visualizes relationships between entities and uses automated enrichers to gather intelligence, storing all data locally for enhanced privacy.
key_features: Automated data enrichment for various entity types (domains, IPs, social media, crypto, etc.)
primary_use_case: Visualizing and investigating relationships between entities for OSINT and cybersecurity reconnaissance.
cover_image_prompt: A digital cartographer meticulously drawing an intricate, glowing web of interconnected nodes and pathways on a large, ancient map. Each node represents a piece of digital information (like an IP address, domain name, or social media profile), and the pathways show the connections discovered. The cartographer uses a magnifying glass that reveals hidden data points and a quill pen that draws glowing lines. Subtle UI elements of the Flowsint application are visible on a nearby digital tablet. The overall mood is one of discovery and clarity, with a dark, mysterious background illuminated by the glowing map. The style should be a detailed digital painting with a focus on intricate linework and ethereal light.
